summary: A superb soundboard mastering captures a 1973 show that shines on Here Comes Sunshine, Ramble On Rose and Row Jimmy.
review: The Jay Ashley‑remastered tape delivers crystal‑clear soundboard fidelity while the band rolls through one of the year’s strongest sets – highlighted by an ecstatic Here Comes Sunshine, a soaring Ramble On Rose and a tear‑inducing Row Jimmy.  Most reviewers rave about the tight first set and superb transitions, noting only occasional harshness in the mix and minor flubs on Morning Dew.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high ratings (mostly 4‑5 stars) praise both the musical performance and the recording quality; only a single mixed comment about harsh sound tempers an otherwise glowing consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the Ashley restoration as crisp, virtually perfect and excellent; one notes a harsh, jarring quality reminiscent of DP‑33, suggesting occasional tonal unevenness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is lauded for its tight, laid‑back urgency and seamless segues; the second set features spectacular transitions (especially Row Jimmy → Jack Straw) but some reviewers feel it dips in consistency toward the end.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for pure surf‑guitar tone on Here Comes Sunshine and overall soaring lead work, with only a few lyrical flubs noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as amazing; his “bombs” and support on Ramble On Rose receive special mention.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals highlighted as edgy on Around/ Brown Eyed Women; occasional shakiness later in the show is noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano work called great, tinkling throughout Ramble On Rose, Row Jimmy and other numbers; his chord insertions are specifically praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy Kreutzmann’s drumming on Jack Straw receives commendation; no specific remarks about Mickey Hart.
